text.title: Knowledge Sharing Ritual Set Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Knowledge Sharing Ritual Set application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Four named rituals: weekly tech-talk (45 min), biweekly demo (30 min), monthly architecture review (90 min), quarterly skip-level (30 min × N). Each has fixed slot.
rule.rationale: Industry consensus (Spotify, Shopify, Stripe): 4 named cadences cover the knowledge-sharing surface.

rule.statement: Every ritual produces an artefact: tech-talk → recording + summary; demo → release notes; arch review → ADR or RFC; skip-level → notes to manager.
rule.rationale: Without artefact, rituals leave only in attendees' heads; artefacts compound.

rule.statement: Every ritual is recorded OR has async write-up. Newcomers + remote / async members consume after.
rule.rationale: Synchronous-only rituals exclude up to 50% of distributed teams; async/recording inclusion is the floor.

rule.statement: Track 'questions newcomers asked' that an existing artefact already answered. >5/month = rituals are not closing the gap.
rule.rationale: Newcomer questions are the leading indicator of tribal knowledge; counter is the decay metric.

rule.statement: Quarterly: review attendance + artefact production + newcomer-question counter. Cut rituals with attendance <40% or artefact rate <50%.
rule.rationale: Rituals accumulate; explicit pruning is the only sustainable equilibrium.
